reasoning: The Lean signature matches the informal statement. "Three times differentiable on [-1,1]" is encoded by the three DifferentiableOn hypotheses (on f, on deriv f, on deriv (deriv f)) over Icc (-1) 1, which is the standard Mathlib pattern for iterated differentiability on a closed interval. The point conditions f(-1)=0, f(0)=0, f(1)=1, f'(0)=0 correspond exactly to hf0, hf1, hf2, hf3. The conclusion ∃ x ∈ Ioo (-1) 1, deriv (deriv (deriv f)) x ≥ 3 faithfully encodes "f^(3)(x) ≥ 3 for some x ∈ (-1,1)"; note that x lies in the open interval, so DifferentiableOn implies DifferentiableAt at x, making `deriv` agree with the true derivative. No hypotheses are silently added or dropped, and no quantifier structure is changed.
